text: The surgeon laparoscopically dissects the colon away from its peritoneal attachments, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon performs limited dissection of the upper pole of the kidney to remove attachments, followed by further dissection of the colon, mobilizing it medially.
A window is created at the left flank, and the ureter and gonadal vein are elevated. The dissection is carried upward towards the renal hilum.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the lower pole, freeing it from surrounding tissues. Careful dissection is performed at the adrenal hilum, separating it from adjacent structures.
The surgeon mobilizes the adrenal gland superiorly, identifies the adrenal vein, and clips and divides it using surgical instruments.
The surgeon meticulously dissects and separates the mass from the undersurface of the liver, ensuring clear separation from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon clips the renal artery using a hemolog and then divides it, completing the vascular control of the kidney.
The surgeon removes dense adhesions surrounding the renal vein and divides the renal vein between clips, ensuring clear separation from surrounding tissues.
The specimen is placed into a retrieval bag and removed through the incision made for extraction.
The surgeon secures complete hemostasis in the surgical field, places an abdominal drain, and closes the prostate.
